items.title: RV Inspection
items.h1: Visual RV Inspection and Moisture Meter Testing in Gatineau-Ottawa
items.serviceType: Visual RV Inspection and Moisture Testing
items.seoTitle: Visual RV Inspection in Gatineau-Ottawa | Seals, Roof and Water Intrusion
items.seoDescription: Visual RV inspection with moisture meter testing in Gatineau-Ottawa. Focus on seals, roof condition and water intrusion. $49.99 in shop, $149.99 mobile.
items.opening: Clinique du VR provides visual RV inspection in Gatineau-Ottawa with moisture meter testing, with a strong focus on seals, roof condition, and signs of water intrusion. Our goal is to quickly identify risk zones, moisture issues, and sealing problems before they lead to more damage.
items.lead: Our inspections are mainly built around seals, roof condition and water intrusion. We do not perform mechanical inspections. We do offer brake maintenance at our shop only, with no mobile brake service.
items.intro: We offer in-shop and mobile visual inspections with a moisture meter to help confirm water-sensitive areas. This service is designed for RVs, travel trailers, and caravans when you want a clearer picture of the overall sealing condition.
items.detailIntro: An effective inspection should focus on what causes the most expensive damage. At Clinique du VR, that means worn seals, weak roof areas, vulnerable openings and signs of water intrusion. The moisture meter helps us confirm suspicious areas and better guide the next steps.
highlights: Focus on seals and roof condition
included: Visual inspection of seals, sealants and openings
Moisture meter readings in risk areas
Visible roof condition and weak points review
Search for signs of water intrusion or moisture
Observation of vulnerable areas around openings and junctions
situations: When you suspect water intrusion
When you want to verify seal or roof condition
Before planning sealing or water-damage repairs
When you want to confirm a suspicious risk area quickly
items.processIntro: Our process is simple and focused on water intrusion. The goal is to confirm visible seal and roof condition, detect moisture in vulnerable areas, and clearly tell you whether repairs should be planned quickly.
process: Initial discussion and inspection type confirmation
Visual inspection of seals, roof and sensitive areas
Moisture meter testing in higher-risk zones
Clear feedback on water intrusion signs and recommended next steps
items.whyChooseTitle: Why choose Clinique du VR for water-intrusion inspection?
items.whyChooseIntro: Water intrusion is one of the most expensive RV problems. A useful inspection should therefore stay focused: confirm seal condition, review the roof and measure moisture in the areas most likely to fail.

whyChoose.title: Water-focused service
whyChoose.text: We keep the inspection focused on the most common water-related failure points instead of turning it into a broad general check.

whyChoose.title: Moisture meter included
whyChoose.text: Using a moisture meter helps confirm suspicious areas and supports the visual findings in higher-risk sections.

whyChoose.title: Clear pricing
whyChoose.text: $49.99 in shop and $149.99 mobile, including a 40 km radius from the office. Contact us for travel beyond that distance.

items.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions about RV inspection

faqs.question: How much does an RV inspection cost?
faqs.answer: The price is $49.99 for an in-shop inspection and $149.99 for a mobile inspection. The mobile rate includes a 40 km radius from the office. Contact us for travel beyond that distance.

faqs.question: What do you mainly check during an inspection?
faqs.answer: We mainly focus on seals, roof condition and signs of water intrusion. We also use a moisture meter in higher-risk areas.

faqs.question: Do you perform mechanical inspections?
faqs.answer: No. We do not perform mechanical inspections, but we do offer brake maintenance at our shop only, with no mobile brake service.

items.title: RV Seal Maintenance
items.h1: Wall and Roof Seal Maintenance in Gatineau-Ottawa
items.serviceType: RV Wall and Roof Seal Maintenance
items.seoTitle: Wall and Roof Seal Maintenance in Gatineau-Ottawa | Clinique du VR
items.seoDescription: Wall and roof seal maintenance in Gatineau-Ottawa to help prevent water intrusion. Service available in shop or mobile.
items.opening: RV maintenance at Clinique du VR focuses on replacing wall seals, adding roof sealant where it is missing, and maintaining TPO or EPDM roof membranes to help prevent water intrusion. We work on the sealing areas and roof surfaces that wear out over time so the vehicle stays better protected against moisture and water damage.
items.lead: Replacing wall seals, adding roof sealant where it is missing, and maintaining TPO or EPDM roof membranes are some of the best ways to help prevent water intrusion. We offer this service in shop or mobile.
items.intro: We handle seal maintenance for walls, roofs, and TPO or EPDM roof membranes to help preserve the watertight condition of the RV, travel trailer, or caravan. The goal is to correct weak water-sensitive areas before they lead to bigger damage.
items.detailIntro: Effective maintenance should focus first on the components that protect the vehicle from water. Wall seals, roof seals, TPO or EPDM roof membranes, and exposed sealing points are often the first areas to wear out. Replacing or maintaining them at the right time helps reduce the risk of water intrusion and the larger repairs that can follow.
included: Replacement of worn wall seals
Roof sealant touch-ups in exposed areas where it is missing
Maintenance of TPO or EPDM roof membranes
Visual review of water-sensitive sections
Preventive maintenance to reduce intrusion risk
Service available in shop or mobile
situations: When seals start drying, cracking or lifting
When you want to prevent water intrusion before the season
When roof or wall sealing shows visible wear
When you want the work done in shop or directly on site
items.processIntro: Our approach is simple: focus on the seals and roof areas that actually protect the vehicle from water. We assess the sealing condition, replace what needs attention and point out the next areas to watch so the RV stays watertight.
process: Review of wall, roof and exposed sealing areas
Seal maintenance or replacement based on condition
Work completed in shop or mobile depending on your situation
Clear feedback on treated areas and what should be monitored next
items.whyChooseTitle: Why choose Clinique du VR for seal maintenance?
items.whyChooseIntro: If you want to avoid water intrusion, you need to act on the right components at the right time. Replacing wall seals, adding roof sealant where it is missing, and maintaining the membrane are essential services for keeping the vehicle watertight and avoiding much more expensive repairs later.

whyChoose.title: Water-focused service
whyChoose.text: We focus on seals and roof areas because they are key to preventing water intrusion.

whyChoose.title: Flexible service
whyChoose.text: The work can be done in the shop or as a mobile visit depending on the situation and the vehicle location.

whyChoose.title: Cost-saving prevention
whyChoose.text: Replacing seals at the right time often costs far less than repairing water damage after an intrusion.

items.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions about RV maintenance

faqs.question: Why replace RV seals?
faqs.answer: Replacing wall seals, adding roof sealant where it is missing, and maintaining the membrane help preserve the watertight condition of the vehicle and reduce the risk of water intrusion before it causes more serious damage.

faqs.question: What maintenance items matter most here?
faqs.answer: Wall seals, roof areas where sealant is missing or failing, roof membranes and other exposed sealing points are some of the most important items to maintain regularly when the goal is water protection.

faqs.question: Do you offer the service in shop or mobile?
faqs.answer: Yes. We offer seal maintenance in shop or as a mobile service, depending on what works best for your situation.

items.seoDescription: Specialized RV repair in Gatineau-Ottawa for water intrusion, mold, roof, roof membrane, floor and wall covering damage. Insurance or private repairs.

items.opening: Clinique du VR provides specialized RV repair in Gatineau-Ottawa for water intrusion, mold, roof, roof membrane, floor and wall covering issues. We handle both insurance-based and private repairs, with a strong focus on identifying the true cause of the damage.
items.lead: When water gets into an RV, the damage often goes beyond the visible surface. The repair approach needs to address the cause, the affected materials and the long-term condition of the vehicle.
items.intro: We carry out specialized repairs on the areas most exposed to water damage, including the roof, roof membrane, floor, walls and interior finish materials.
items.detailIntro: Water intrusion can affect structure, interior materials and the overall condition of the RV. A stain, mold odour, soft floor or damaged wall covering can point to a more advanced issue that requires methodical repair work.
included: Investigation of the true leak source
Treatment of moisture-affected or mold-affected areas
Roof and roof membrane repair
Repair or replacement of damaged flooring
Wall covering repair and interior restoration
Insurance or private repairs depending on your file

Damaged roof or roof membrane
Damaged floor, ceiling or wall covering
A repair file that is insurance-based or private
items.processIntro: Our specialized repair method starts by confirming the source of the damage, then follows a logical restoration order. That makes it possible to correct the real issue and adapt the work whether the file is insurance-based or private.
process: Diagnosis to confirm the source of the leak or damage
Assessment of affected areas such as the roof, floor or walls
Repair and restoration based on the scope of the file
Clear follow-up for insurance or private repair work
items.whyChooseTitle: Why choose Clinique du VR for specialized repair?
items.whyChooseIntro: The difference between a durable repair and a surface-level fix comes down to diagnosis quality, execution standards and the ability to treat water-related damage as a complete system.

whyChoose.title: Cause-first diagnosis
whyChoose.text: We work to find the true origin of the water intrusion before rebuilding or closing damaged sections.

items.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions about RV repair

faqs.question: What should I do if my RV has a water leak?
faqs.answer: It should be checked quickly so the source can be confirmed before more damage reaches the structure, floor, walls, roof membrane and interior materials, while also reducing the risk of mold.

faqs.question: Do you repair roofs and roof membranes?
faqs.answer: Yes. We perform targeted roof and roof membrane repairs when appropriate, depending on material condition and how extensive the damage is.

faqs.question: Do you handle insurance and private repairs?
faqs.answer: Yes. We handle both insurance-based and private repairs depending on the nature of the damage and the needs of the file.

The goal is to reduce uncertainty, improve decision quality and help both buyers and sellers move forward with more confidence.

items.whyChooseTitle: Why choose Clinique du VR for RV buying and selling support?

items.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions about RV buying and selling support

faqs.question: Why inspect an RV before buying it?
faqs.answer: A pre-purchase inspection helps reveal roof, seal, water intrusion and system issues that can strongly affect real value and future repair costs.

faqs.question: Which repairs are worth doing before selling an RV?
faqs.answer: The most useful work is the work that improves trust, clarifies condition and strengthens presentation without overinvesting in low-impact changes.

faqs.question: Can you help if I am not sure whether to repair or sell?
faqs.answer: Yes. This service is designed to help owners prioritize the most meaningful corrections and choose between repair, sale or a more gradual upgrade path.
